description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous artisan workshop (atelier) interior, rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO triptych frames, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders between any parts — strictly forbidden to split the image into thirds or sections; the result must read as a single oil-painted scroll-like view of the same room.

IMPORTANT compositional constraint: this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally, snapping to one of three viewing windows. Each viewing window shows roughly a third of the image (0–33%, 33–66%, 66–100% of the width). For this reason, NO key freestanding element (no door, no workbench, no shelf, no architectural feature) may straddle the vertical position at 33% of the image. The area around 33% must contain ONLY plain continuous wall surface — it is the 'breathing zone' where viewing window 1 transitions to viewing window 2.

Architecture (described continuously across the width): the viewer looks into a warm artisan's workshop with three walls visible. At the far LEFT (roughly 0–18%), a LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. From 18% to 82% we see the FLAT BACK WALL straight on. From 82% to the right edge, a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. The walls meet at TWO interior corners (around 18% and 82%) — visible only as a soft change of wall plane and gentle painted shading at the join, never as a hard line. ONE continuous old wide-plank wooden floor runs unbroken across the bottom, slightly worn and uneven. ONE continuous exposed wooden ceiling beam runs unbroken across the top. The walls are warm aged plaster with occasional exposed vertical wooden framing posts (timber studs).

Feature details by horizontal position (0% = left edge, 100% = right edge):

• 0–28% — SECTION 1 (LEFT): on the BACK WALL, a WOODEN GARDEN DOOR centered around 14%, painted in a soft sage-green tone with old patina, brass handle, and glass panels in the upper half. Through the glass and (if open) through the door opening we see a SUNLIT GARDEN view: rosebushes in full bloom, climbing vines on a low stone wall, a paved garden path, distant chestnut trees, warm afternoon sunlight filtering through foliage. Painted in the same Art Déco line-art + soft wash style as the rest of the panorama. Immediately in front of the door, a small worn rope doormat lies on the floor. The wooden floor around the door is otherwise EMPTY (no furniture, no objects). A clean stretch of plain plaster wall sits between the door frame and the 33% mark.

• 33% area — only plain continuous plaster wall (BREATHING ZONE for the swipe boundary between Section 1 and Section 2). No door frame, no window, no furniture, no decorative element here.

• 36–63% — SECTION 2 (CENTER): on the BACK WALL, a substantial WOODEN WORKBENCH (établi) running flush along the back wall, ~1.6m wide, with a thick worn-oak top, sturdy square legs, and a low cross-bar between the legs. The workbench top is COMPLETELY EMPTY — no tools, no objects, no clutter — just bare worn oak ready to receive composited items later. Above the workbench, the LEFT HALF of a wide WOODEN WINDOW with its windowsill resting just a few centimetres above the workbench top. The window features an Art Nouveau wooden frame: the upper portion is a decorative IMPOSTE (transom window) with curved, organic, stylized Art Nouveau wooden mullions forming gentle whiplash curves and stylized plant tendrils. The main panes below the imposte are divided by straight vertical wooden mullions. Through the glass we see the SAME SUNLIT GARDEN as Section 1 continuing: rosebushes, climbing vines, stone wall, paved path, chestnut foliage. A polished wooden WINDOW LEDGE / TABLETTE (tablette en bois) runs along the interior side of the windowsill, slightly protruding into the room, intended to hold flower pots — but the tablette is COMPLETELY EMPTY for now.

• 66% — the wide back-wall WINDOW continues uninterrupted across this position. The window's central vertical wooden frame stile (a sturdy structural mullion painted the same wood tone as the rest of the frame) is positioned EXACTLY at the 66% horizontal mark of the full image. This central stile is the only feature allowed at 66% — it serves as the natural visual break between Section 2 and Section 3, treated as a clean structural division. The Art Nouveau imposte above continues uninterrupted (a curving organic motif crosses the central stile gracefully). No other freestanding element is placed near this stripe.

• 69–88% — SECTION 3 (RIGHT): on the BACK WALL, the RIGHT HALF of the same wide window continues, with the same Art Nouveau imposte above and straight vertical mullions below, and the EMPTY wooden tablette continuing along the interior sill. Underneath this right window half, a SMALL WOODEN WORKBENCH (petit établi) — narrower than the central one, ~0.9m wide, similar worn-oak top and sturdy legs, also COMPLETELY EMPTY (bare wood, no tools, no objects). Immediately to the right of the small workbench, against the back wall and the angled right side wall, TWO HORIZONTAL EMPTY WOODEN SHELVES (étagères en bois) fixed to the wall at chest and shoulder height, naked unfinished pine, holding NOTHING. The shelves are wide enough to display crafts and small objects later.

• 82–100% — RIGHT SIDE WALL angling forward, partly behind the shelves. Plain warm plaster with one or two exposed vertical wooden framing posts. A length of weathered burlap or rough canvas drop-cloth loosely draped over a wooden hook on the side wall, hanging idle. Floor here is empty wide-plank wood — no furniture, no objects, leaving generous bare floor space.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left (coming from the back-wall window and the partly-open garden door), consistent across the whole panorama. Cohesive Art Déco rendering: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain. No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINTS:
(a) All garden views (through the door at left and through the wide window at center/right) show the SAME continuous garden seen at slightly different angles — rosebushes, climbing vines, stone wall, paved path, chestnut foliage, warm afternoon light. Consistent palette and brushwork between views.
(b) No transparent areas anywhere — the PNG is fully opaque.
(c) No vertical seams, no black bars, no panel borders anywhere.
(d) Workbenches, tablette, and shelves are COMPLETELY EMPTY — no tools, no objects, no clutter, no jars, no pots, no flowers. Just bare wood waiting for composited items.
(e) The wide back-wall window's central wooden mullion stile is at EXACTLY the 66% horizontal of the full image and is the only feature allowed at that vertical position.
(f) Nothing crosses the 33% mark — it is plain plaster wall.
